note: All learners must take the two mandatory Pure Core papers and any two of the optional papers.

papers.description: Assesses content from pure core and all of the overarching themes.
Assesses content from pure core and all of the overarching themes.
Assesses content from statistics and all of the overarching themes.
Assesses content from mechanics and all of the overarching themes.
Assesses content from discrete mathematics and all of the overarching themes.
Assesses content from additional pure mathematics and all of the overarching themes.

topics.name: Mathematical Proof
subtopics: Construct proofs using mathematical induction
Proof by contradiction
Proof by direct argument
Use of counterexamples
Understand and use the language of mathematical proof

topics.name: Complex Numbers
subtopics: Understand and use the complex number system
Perform arithmetic operations on complex numbers
Use the complex conjugate
Represent complex numbers on an Argand diagram
Convert between Cartesian and modulus-argument form
Understand and use de Moivre's theorem
Find nth roots of complex numbers
Use complex numbers to solve polynomial equations

topics.name: Matrices
subtopics: Understand and use matrix algebra
Add, subtract and multiply conformable matrices
Multiply a matrix by a scalar
Understand and use zero and identity matrices
Calculate the determinant of 2x2 and 3x3 matrices
Find and use the inverse of non-singular matrices
Use matrices to represent linear transformations in 2D
Use matrices to solve systems of linear equations

topics.name: Further Algebra and Functions
subtopics: Understand and use the relationship between roots and coefficients of polynomial equations
Form a polynomial equation whose roots are a linear transformation of the roots of a given equation
Understand and use partial fractions with repeated linear factors
Understand and use summation formulae for sums of squares and cubes

topics.name: Further Calculus
subtopics: Differentiate and integrate inverse trigonometric functions
Derive and use reduction formulae for integration
Understand and use improper integrals
Differentiate and integrate hyperbolic functions
Understand and use the inverse hyperbolic functions

topics.name: Further Vectors
subtopics: Understand and use the vector and Cartesian forms of an equation of a straight line in 3D
Understand and use the vector and Cartesian forms of the equation of a plane
Calculate the scalar product and use it to calculate angles between lines and planes
Find the intersection of a line and a plane
Calculate the perpendicular distance from a point to a plane

topics.name: Polar Coordinates
subtopics: Understand and use polar coordinates
Convert between polar and Cartesian coordinates
Sketch curves with equations given in polar form
Find the area enclosed by a polar curve

topics.name: Differential Equations
subtopics: Find and use an integrating factor to solve differential equations of the form dy/dx + P(x)y = Q(x)
Solve differential equations of the form y'' + ay' + by = 0
Solve differential equations of the form y'' + ay' + by = f(x)
Understand the terms complementary function and particular integral
Solve systems of first order differential equations

topics.name: Probability and Distributions
subtopics: Understand and use conditional probability
Understand and use the normal distribution
Understand and use the Poisson distribution
Understand and use the continuous uniform distribution
Understand and use the exponential distribution
Understand and use linear combinations of random variables
Understand and use the t-distribution

topics.name: Statistical Inference
subtopics: Understand and use unbiased estimators of variance
Understand and use confidence intervals
Carry out hypothesis tests for the mean of a normal distribution with unknown variance
Carry out hypothesis tests for the difference between means of two normal distributions
Carry out chi-squared tests (goodness of fit and contingency tables)
Understand and use correlation and regression

topics.name: Dimensional Analysis
subtopics: Understand and use dimensions of physical quantities
Use dimensional analysis to check the validity of physical relationships
Understand the limitations of dimensional analysis

topics.name: Work, Energy and Power
subtopics: Understand and use the concept of work done by a force
Understand and use the principle of conservation of energy
Understand and use kinetic energy and potential energy (gravitational and elastic)
Understand and use power
Solve problems involving work, energy and power

topics.name: Impulse and Momentum
subtopics: Understand and use the concept of linear momentum
Understand and use impulse
Understand and use the principle of conservation of linear momentum
Understand and use Newton's experimental law for collisions
Solve problems involving direct impact of elastic particles

topics.name: Circular Motion
subtopics: Understand and use the terminology of circular motion
Understand and use angular speed
Understand that the acceleration is towards the centre of the circle
Use Newton's second law applied to circular motion
Model motion in a horizontal circle and a vertical circle

topics.name: Centre of Mass and Moments
subtopics: Understand and use the centre of mass of a rigid body
Find the centre of mass of a composite body
Solve problems involving equilibrium of rigid bodies
Understand and use the moment of inertia
Understand and use angular momentum

topics.name: Graphs and Networks
subtopics: Understand the terminology of graphs including nodes, arcs, trees and planar graphs
Use adjacency matrices and distance tables
Solve problems using Eulerian and Hamiltonian graphs
Use the planarity algorithm
Use graphs to model real-world problems

topics.name: Network Algorithms
subtopics: Use Kruskal's and Prim's algorithms to find minimum connectors
Use Dijkstra's algorithm to find the shortest path
Use the route inspection algorithm
Use the travelling salesperson problem algorithms
Apply network algorithms to solve problems

topics.name: Linear Programming
subtopics: Formulate linear programming problems
Solve linear programming problems using graphical methods
Use the simplex algorithm
Understand and use the concept of degeneracy
Formulate and solve allocation problems

topics.name: Game Theory
subtopics: Understand and use zero-sum games
Solve simple games using dominance arguments
Solve games using mixed strategies
Use game theory to model real-world situations

topics.name: Number Theory
subtopics: Understand and use divisibility tests
Understand and use the division algorithm
Use the Euclidean algorithm to find highest common factors
Understand and use modular arithmetic
Understand and use Fermat's little theorem
Understand and use the Chinese remainder theorem

topics.name: Further Group Theory
subtopics: Understand and use the axioms of a group
Understand and use the language of group theory (identity, inverse, order, closure)
Understand and use Lagrange's theorem
Understand and use cyclic groups
Understand and use isomorphism

topics.name: Further Vectors and Geometry
subtopics: Understand and use vector algebra in 2D and 3D
Use the scalar product and vector product
Use vectors to solve geometric problems
Understand and use the equation of lines and planes in vector form

topics.name: Sequences and Series
subtopics: Understand and use the method of differences
Use Maclaurin series
Use standard series expansions
Derive series expansions of compound functions
